title: Euclid's Proposition 1: Equilateral Triangle
description:
Construct an equilateral triangle on a given line segment AB.

Given the endpoints A=(ax, ay) and B=(bx, by) of segment AB, construct an equilateral triangle on it using Euclid's compass-and-straightedge method:
1. Draw circle C1 centered at A with radius AB.
2. Draw circle C2 centered at B with radius AB.
3. Their intersection gives point C (the apex).
4. Return the three vertices [A, B, C] as `Point` objects.

The injected primitives are `Point(x, y)`, `Circle(center, radius)`, and `intersect(shapeA, shapeB) → Point[]`.
hints: A compass is a Circle in Euclid's constructions. Set the radius to the length of AB.
Use Math.sqrt((bx-ax)**2 + (by-ay)**2) to compute the side length, then Circle(A, side) and Circle(B, side).
intersect(circleA, circleB) returns an array of Point objects. The apex is pts[0].
